variables.name: 0-reducible
variables.definition: A Runge–Kutta method (A, b, c) is 0-reducible if the stage index set can be partitioned into two subsets {1, 2, ..., s} = P0 ∪ P1 such that bi = 0 for all i ∈ P0 and such that aij = 0 if i ∈ P1 and j ∈ P0.

variables.name: P-reducible
variables.definition: A Runge–Kutta method (A, b, c) is P-reducible if the stage index set can be partitioned into {1, 2, ..., s} = P1 ∪ P2 ∪ ... ∪ Ps and if, for all I, J = 1, 2, ..., s, the sum over j in PJ of aij is constant for all i in PI.

variables.name: 0-reduced method

The group G1/(1+Hp) has cosets representing methods that agree to O(h^{p+1}) in one step, with E(1+Hp) being the coset of methods that match the exact solution to that order. preamble: The definition concerns a general linear method represented by matrices (A, U, B, V). The method is assumed to have p = q and s = r = p + 1, with A being diagonally implicit and V having a specific block structure. The concept of inherent Runge-Kutta stability imposes conditions on the matrices BA - XB and BU - XV + VX.
